input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling, which come via VS or the Build Tools:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# During install or after, add these workload components:
# - "ASP.NET and web development" (optional but common)
# - ".NET Multi-platform App UI development"
# - "Universal Windows Platform development" (for WinUI/XAML tooling)

# Or modify from command line:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload installs a bundled Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and verify paths:

```powershell
# Find where the MAUI workload put the Android SDK
$androidSdkPath = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdkPath)) {
    # Fallback: check the dotnet MAUI manifest for the path
    $androidSdkPath = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".android\sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdkPath"

# List installed SDK platforms and build-tools
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

If the bundled SDK is missing or incomplete:

```powershell
# Install Android command-line tools manually
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools

# Or download directly
$cmdlineUrl = "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip"
Invoke-WebRequest $cmdlineUrl -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools"

# Install required components (discover latest versions dynamically)
$sdkmanager = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List available platforms to pick the right API level
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install what MAUI needs (API 35 is current as of early 2026 — confirm from list above)
& $sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

## Step 5 — Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Persist ANDROID_HOME
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
    "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")

# Add platform-tools to PATH (for adb)
$currentPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("PATH", "User")
$platformTools = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\platform-tools"
if ($currentPath -notlike "*$platformTools*") {
    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("PATH",
        "$currentPath;$platformTools", "User")
}

# Reload in current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "User")
$env:PATH = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("PATH", "User")
```

## Step 6 — Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
$sdkmanager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"
$avdmanager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# Discover available system images
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "system-images;android-.*google_apis;x86_64"

# Install a system image (pick latest stable from the list above)
& $sdkmanager "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create the AVD
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel_7"

# Launch (optional — test it works)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## Step 7 — Enable Windows Developer Mode (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Check current status
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock"

# Enable Developer Mode
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 8 — Verify Everything

```powershell
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiSmokeTest -o "$env:TEMP\MauiSmokeTest"
cd "$env:TEMP\MauiSmokeTest"

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (emulator must be running)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -t:Run

# Clean up
Remove-Item -Recurse "$env:TEMP\MauiSmokeTest"
```

---

**Key dynamic-discovery commands to remember:**

| What you need | Command |
|---|---|
| Latest .NET SDK | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK` |
| Available MAUI workloads | `dotnet workload search maui` |
| Android API levels | `sdkmanager --list \| Select-String "platforms;android-"` |
| System images | `sdkmanager --list \| Select-String "system-images"` |
| Target framework names | `dotnet new maui --dry-run` then check the `.csproj` `<TargetFrameworks>` |

Adjust the `net9.0-*` TFMs if you installed a different SDK version. The `--list` commands in steps 4 and 6 are how you discover the right versions at install time rather than hardcoding them.
